what_it_does: Mounts a fixed, bottom-right draggable dock that wraps a brand icon (the V) and toggles a placeholder AI assistant panel, injecting its own CSS and exposing a mount function plus a global DNA namespace helper.
observations: Imports createBrandIcon and BRAND_ICON_CSS from a sibling brand-icon.js module.
Injects a single shared <style> element containing both brand-icon and dock/panel CSS.
The dock is a 46x46 px fixed wrapper; the icon inside owns its own hover/focus transforms.
Implements threshold-based drag detection (4 px total travel) before setting pointer capture.
Uses a capture-phase click listener on the wrapper to swallow the trailing click after a drag, preserving the icon's bubble-phase tap handler.
The panel is positioned relative to the icon's current bounds and repositions during drag and on resize.
Panel content is a placeholder explicitly noting the design is the next step.
Also assigns window.DNA.mountVeeDock for non-module callers.
standouts: Thresholded tap-vs-drag pointer handling with capture-phase click swallowing.
Panel follows the dragged icon rather than staying at a static anchor.
Explicit placeholder UI with a note that the real AI panel design comes next.
conventions: Returns a mount API object with open, close, toggle, and destroy methods.
CSS is self-contained and injected once, using CSS custom properties with literal fallbacks.
Default container is document.body; fixed positioning with explicit z-index.
Provides both ES module export and global namespace exposure.
concerns: swallowClick is set true after a drag but only cleared by a subsequent click event, so a drag with no click could leave the flag set and suppress the next legitimate tap.
Pointer capture is only acquired after the drag threshold, so fast movements that leave the wrapper before threshold may stop receiving pointermove events.
destroy removes the dock and panel from the DOM but does not explicitly tear down the brand icon's internal listeners or the injected style element.
The dock wrapper lacks accessibility attributes such as role, aria-label, or keyboard handlers.
notes: This is the surface-side glue that turns the V brand icon into a draggable, panel-opening AI access point while keeping the icon's own click behavior intact.
questions: What exact API does createBrandIcon expose (getBounds, setFocusActive, event handlers)?
What does BRAND_ICON_CSS contain and how does it interact with the dock's sizing assumptions?
How will the placeholder panel content be replaced by the final AI assistant design?
Is the drag offset meant to persist across page reloads or sessions?
How are non-module callers expected to load this script before calling window.DNA.mountVeeDock?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a live, bottom-right, draggable V dock that opens a placeholder AI panel.
apparent_themes: Composition-to-surface merge without redrawing the underlying component.
Gesture disambiguation to preserve nested interactive elements.
Explicit design placeholders as first-class implementation artifacts.
intention_signals: It appears intended to be the first tangible surface integration point for an AI assistant identity, letting users drag a branded icon aside and open a right-hand-man panel while keeping the icon component itself untouched.
novelty: The combination of a thresholded wrapper drag, capture-phase trailing-click suppression, and a panel that tracks the dragged icon's live bounds is the distinctive interaction pattern here.
contribution: A reusable dock wrapper that adds draggable positioning, tap-vs-drag disambiguation, a toggleable placeholder panel, and global-namespace exposure around an externally supplied brand icon.
summary_for_embedding: vee-dock.js is a surface component that mounts a fixed, draggable, bottom-right dock wrapping the V brand icon and toggles a placeholder AI assistant panel with threshold-based gesture handling and CSS injection.
intention_for_embedding: The file is meant to wire composition's V brand icon into the DNA surface as a persistent, draggable access point for an integrated AI assistant panel.

symbols.does: Injects the combined brand-icon and dock/panel CSS into the document head once.
symbols.description: Creates a <style id="vee-dock-css"> element containing BRAND_ICON_CSS plus VEE_DOCK_CSS and appends it to document.head, guarded by a module flag and an existing-id check to avoid duplicate injection.

symbols.does: Mounts the draggable V dock and AI panel into a container and returns a control API.
symbols.description: Creates the panel and dock DOM elements, instantiates the brand icon inside the dock, wires open/close/toggle logic, attaches pointer drag handlers with a tap threshold, positions the panel relative to the icon, and exposes the result as an object with open, close, toggle, and destroy methods.

symbols.does: Calculates and sets the panel's fixed position based on the brand icon's current bounds.
symbols.description: Reads brand.getBounds(), aligns the panel's right edge with the icon's right edge, places it above the icon with a gap, falls back to below the icon when viewport space is tight, and clamps horizontally into the viewport.

symbols.does: Reveals the AI panel and activates the brand icon's focus-active state.
symbols.description: Sets isOpen to true, unhides the panel, calls positionPanel to place it next to the icon, and invokes brand.setFocusActive(true) so the icon shows the active composition state.

symbols.does: Hides the AI panel and deactivates the brand icon's focus-active state.
symbols.description: Sets isOpen to false, hides the panel with the hidden attribute, and invokes brand.setFocusActive(false) to remove the active state from the icon.

symbols.does: Opens the panel if closed, otherwise closes it.
symbols.description: A thin convenience function that switches between the open and close states based on the current isOpen flag.

symbols.does: Finalizes a pointer interaction and marks trailing clicks to be swallowed after a drag.
symbols.description: Called on pointerup or pointercancel; if dragging was active it removes the dragging class, releases pointer capture, and sets swallowClick true so the capture-phase click guard can prevent the trailing click from reaching the icon.

symbols.does: Repositions the open panel when the viewport is resized.
symbols.description: A window resize listener that calls positionPanel only while the panel is open, keeping the panel anchored to the icon after layout changes.

symbols.does: Removes the resize listener and detaches the dock and panel from the DOM.
symbols.description: Cleans up the window resize handler, then removes both the dock wrapper and the panel element from their host container.
